title: Hand Rankings
subtitle: From high card to royal flush — knowing what beats what
intro: Every poker decision eventually reduces to comparing five-card hands. Texas Hold'em uses a fixed hierarchy: high card, one pair, two pair, three of a kind, straight, flush, full house, four of a kind, straight flush, and royal flush.

You always form the best five-card hand from exactly five cards — using zero, one, or both hole cards plus community cards. When two players tie on the rank of a hand, kickers (the side cards) or the board itself decide the winner.

Misreading hand strength is one of the costliest beginner mistakes. This lesson trains you to rank hands quickly, spot counterfeited pairs, and recognize when the board alone is the best hand for everyone.

explanations.A: A straight beats three of a kind but loses to flush and full house.
explanations.B: A flush beats straight and three of a kind but loses to full house.
explanations.C: Correct — full house (trips + pair) beats flush, straight, and three of a kind.
explanations.D: Three of a kind is strong but below straight and flush.

explanations.A: Two pair loses to flush.
explanations.B: Correct — full house beats flush.

remediation.question: Rank these from lowest to highest: flush, straight, full house.

explanations.A: Flush beats straight.
explanations.B: Correct — straight, then flush, then full house.
explanations.C: Full house is highest of the three.

questions.question: A royal flush is:
options: Five cards of the same suit any ranks
A♥ K♦ Q♣ J♠ T♥ mixed suits

explanations.A: Correct — A-K-Q-J-T all the same suit is the royal flush, the highest possible hand.
explanations.B: Any same-suit five cards is a flush; royal requires the top five ranks.
explanations.C: Mixed suits cannot form a flush.
explanations.D: Four aces is four of a kind, not a royal flush.

remediation.question: The best possible Hold'em hand is:

explanations.A: Four of a kind is beaten by straight flush.
explanations.B: Correct — royal flush (A-high straight flush) is unbeatable.
explanations.C: Full house loses to straight flush.
explanations.D: King-high flush is far from the nuts.

explanations.A: Correct — five consecutive clubs is a straight flush.
explanations.B: Missing the ten for a straight.
explanations.C: Nine of hearts breaks the flush.
explanations.D: Two pair, not a straight flush.

questions.question: Board: K♠ 7♥ 7♦ 3♣ 3♠. You hold K♦ Q♣. Your best hand is:
options: Full house, sevens full of threes

explanations.A: K-K-7-7-3 uses kings and threes with a seven kicker.
explanations.B: Correct — kings and threes with a seven kicker: K-K-3-3-7.
explanations.C: Full house would need trips + pair; you have two pair only.
explanations.D: Two pair is better than one pair.

remediation.question: You have A♠ A♥, board 9♣ 9♦ 5♠ 2♥ K♣. Best hand:

explanations.A: Correct — A-A-9-9-K beats using only board pairs.
explanations.B: Nines and fives ignores your aces.
explanations.C: No trips on board for a boat.

remediation.question: Two pair means:
options: Two different pairs among your five cards
Two players each have a pair

explanations.A: Correct — e.g. K-K-7-7-x is two pair.
explanations.B: Hole cards need not both pair.
explanations.D: Board pairs alone can make two pair for you.

questions.question: You: A♦ J♦. Villain: A♠ T♠. Board: A♥ 8♣ 5♦ 2♠ K♣. Who wins?

explanations.A: Correct — both have pair of aces; your J kicker beats T.
explanations.B: Villain's ten is lower than your jack.
explanations.D: Only one pair of aces matters.

remediation.question: Same pair on board, you have K kicker, opponent Q kicker. Winner:

explanations.A: Correct — higher kicker wins when pairs tie.
explanations.B: Queen loses to king.

remediation.question: Kickers matter when:
options: Both players have the same hand rank

explanations.A: Correct — tied hand ranks compare side cards.
explanations.B: Flush rank is decided by highest card in flush.
explanations.C: Paired boards can still need kickers.

questions.question: Board: Q♠ J♠ T♠ 9♠ 8♠. All players checked to showdown. Result?
options: Player with A♠ in hole wins
Split pot — everyone plays the board

explanations.A: A spade in hole doesn't improve on a straight flush board.
explanations.B: Correct — the board is a straight flush; hole cards irrelevant.
explanations.C: Hole cards don't matter when board is best for all.

remediation.question: When the board is the best five for all players:

explanations.A: Hole cards don't beat the board if board is best five.
explanations.B: Correct — tied best hand splits.

remediation.question: You may use zero hole cards when:
options: The board alone beats any combo with your cards

explanations.A: Correct — 'playing the board' uses zero hole cards.
explanations.B: Folded players don't showdown.
explanations.C: Aces might still play if board isn't best alone.
explanations.D: Zero hole cards is legal when board is strongest.

explanations.A: Correct — three kings and two sevens is a full house.
explanations.B: Two pair only — need three of one rank.
explanations.C: Four sevens is four of a kind.

explanations.A: Correct — 'boat' is common slang for full house.
explanations.B: Wheel is A-2-3-4-5 straight.

explanations.A: Two pair needs two ranks paired, not three eights.
explanations.B: Correct — eights full of kings.
explanations.C: Trips is three of a kind without a pair.
explanations.D: Four of a kind needs four eights.

explanations.A: Correct — wheel: ace plays low as 1 in A-2-3-4-5.
explanations.B: Nine of hearts breaks the straight.
explanations.C: Gap at ten — not five consecutive ranks.
explanations.D: Even ranks skip — not a straight.

explanations.A: Ace-high is Broadway, different straight.
explanations.B: Correct — ace can be low in the wheel.
explanations.D: Ace is part of the straight, not kicker.

explanations.A: Flush beats straight.
explanations.B: Correct — straight beats three of a kind.

questions.question: You hold 2♠ 3♠. Board: A♠ K♠ 7♥ 5♠ 9♦. Your hand is:

explanations.A: Pair of aces ignores the five spades available.
explanations.B: Correct — A-K-9-5-2 spades is an ace-high flush.
explanations.C: You completed the flush, not just a draw.

remediation.question: Flush tie is broken by:
options: Highest card in the flush, then next cards

explanations.A: Correct — compare top down through all five flush cards.
explanations.B: Suit of hole cards doesn't matter for tie.

remediation.question: Five hearts on board, you have no hearts. You:
options: Have high card only if board flush is best

explanations.A: You don't hold a heart but board gives everyone a flush.
explanations.B: Correct — you play the board flush; kickers may tie.
explanations.C: High card only applies when the board isn't a completed flush.

questions.question: You hold 8♦ 8♠. Board: 8♥ K♣ K♦ 5♠ 5♥. Your effective hand vs someone with A♠ K♠?
options: They win — kings full of fives beat eights full

explanations.A: You have eights full but they have kings full of eights.
explanations.B: Correct — higher trips in full house wins: K-K-K-8-8 beats 8-8-8-K-K.
explanations.D: Trip eights became second best after board paired.

remediation.question: Counterfeiting often happens when:
options: Board pairs and reduces your pocket pair's value

explanations.A: Correct — board pairs can 'counterfeit' your pocket pair.
explanations.B: Flush improves your hand.

remediation.question: Pocket tens on board T-K-K-5-5 makes:
options: Weaker full house than K-x with a king

explanations.A: Tens full loses to kings full.
explanations.B: Correct — opponent with a king has kings full of tens.

questions.question: Board: 9♠ 8♠ 7♦ 6♣ 5♥. Player A: A♠ 2♠. Player B: T♦ T♣. Winner?

explanations.A: A has 9-high straight (9-8-7-6-5); T makes ten-high straight.
explanations.B: Correct — T-9-8-7-6 beats 9-8-7-6-5.
explanations.D: No five spades for a flush.

remediation.question: Ten-high straight on 9-8-7-6-5 board needs:
options: A ten in hole or as part of best five

explanations.A: Correct — T completes T-9-8-7-6.
explanations.B: Suits irrelevant for straight.
explanations.D: Ace low is wheel, different board.

remediation.question: When both have straights, winner has:
options: Higher top card of the straight

explanations.A: Straights can differ in high card.
explanations.B: Correct — compare the highest card of the straight.

explanations.A: Pair and two pair lose to straight.
explanations.B: Two pair loses to straight.

explanations.A: Correct.
explanations.B: That's any flush.

placementQuestions.question: Same pair, you A-kicker, foe Q-kicker:

explanations.A: Correct — ace kicker.
explanations.B: Queen is lower.

placementQuestions.question: Board is best five for all:

explanations.A: Correct — play the board.
explanations.B: Button doesn't auto-win.

explanations.A: Two pair is weaker.
explanations.B: Correct.

explanations.A: Broadway.
explanations.B: Correct — ace low.

explanations.A: Correct.
explanations.B: Boat beats flush.

placementQuestions.question: Higher straight wins when:
options: Top card of straight is higher

explanations.A: Correct.
explanations.B: Doesn't matter how many hole cards.
